description_long: This structure is a traditional building with a steeply pitched, thatched roof, reminiscent of historical Japanese or East Asian rural architecture. The roof is dark gray and extends well beyond the walls, with visible wooden crossbeams at the apex and corners, suggesting a timber frame construction. The walls are light beige with dark wooden framing, arranged in a grid pattern, and the base is a solid, slightly raised blue-gray foundation. Minimal exterior ornamentation is present, emphasizing functional simplicity and historical authenticity. Small rectangular features on the lower wall may represent utility boxes or plaques. The overall appearance is that of a preserved or reconstructed historical attraction, likely found in a heritage park or open-air museum.
description: A traditional building with a steep, dark thatched roof, timber-framed beige walls, and a solid blue-gray base, evoking historical East Asian rural architecture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the steep roof, timber-framed beige wall, and small rectangular features at the lower left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, overhanging roof and the uninterrupted grid pattern of the wall.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but without the small rectangular features on the wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the extended roof and grid wall, with a few small rectangular features at the base.
